name: HEX_CONFIG_PARAM_FEATURES_B
description: The base table stores the details of the features/parameters used in the extract configuration groups.

columns.Name: PARAM_FEATURE_ID
columns.Comments: System generated Primary Key. Uniquely identifies a record.

columns.Name: MAX_VALUE
columns.Comments: This column contains the Maximum Value of the Feature/Parameter

columns.Name: PARAM_FEATURE_RECOMMENDED_VAL
columns.Comments: This column contains the recommended value for parameters and features

columns.Name: MIN_VALUE
columns.Comments: This column contains the Minimum Value of the feature/Parameter

columns.Name: BASE_PARAM_FEATURE_CODE
columns.Comments: The column indicates the name for the feature/parameter. Used for migrating the seed data and for storing the custom code.

columns.Name: PARAM_FEATURE_DEFAULT_VALUE
columns.Comments: The column indicates the value for the feature/parameter.

columns.Name: PARAM_FEATURE_TYPE
columns.Comments: The column indicates the type. Valid values are FEATURE, PARAMETER.

columns.Name: LEGACY_PARAM_FEATURE_CODE
columns.Comments: The column indicates the reference lookup code for deriving the value for the specific feature/parameter.

columns.Name: LEGACY_COMPATIBLE
columns.Comments: The column indicates the parameter compatibility with the values LEGACY/NEW/BOTH.

columns.Name: MANDATORY_FLAG
columns.Comments: The column indicates if the parameter/feature is mandatory. Valid values are Y and N.

columns.Name: ENABLED_FLAG
columns.Comments: ENABLED/HIDE/DEPRECATED/DISABLED (Default :ENABLED)

columns.Name: PARAM_FEAT_LOOKUP_TYPE
columns.Comments: The column indicates the lookup associated with the parameter/feature.

columns.Name: ATTRIBUTE_CATEGORY
columns.Comments: Descriptive Flexfield: structure definition of the user descriptive flexfield.

columns.Name: ATTRIBUTE1
columns.Comments: Descriptive Flexfield: segment of the user descriptive flexfield.

columns.Name: ATTRIBUTE2
columns.Comments: Descriptive Flexfield: segment of the user descriptive flexfield.

columns.Name: ATTRIBUTE3
columns.Comments: Descriptive Flexfield: segment of the user descriptive flexfield.

columns.Name: ATTRIBUTE4
columns.Comments: Descriptive Flexfield: segment of the user descriptive flexfield.

columns.Name: ATTRIBUTE5
columns.Comments: Descriptive Flexfield: segment of the user descriptive flexfield.

columns.Name: ENTERPRISE_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier of Enterprise, used for multi-tenancy partitioning. Foreign key to HR_ORGANIZATION_UNITS.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: MODULE_ID
columns.Comments: Seed Data Framework: indicates the module that owns the row. A module is an entry in Application Taxonomy such as a Logical Business Area. When the MODULE_ID column exists and the owner of the row is not specified, then the Seed Data Framework will not extract the row as seed data.

columns.Name: SGUID
columns.Comments: The seed global unique identifier. Oracle internal use only.

columns.Name: SEED_DATA_SOURCE
columns.Comments: Source of seed data record. A value of 'BULK_SEED_DATA_SCRIPT' indicates that record was bulk loaded. Otherwise, specifies the name of the seed data file.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET1
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET1. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET2
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET2. Context values are Y or N.
